last_analyst_note:
Mode 1b (Prediction Ingestion — PRED-106) @ 2026-04-29T16:05:00.000Z.

DISPATCHER TRACE (clean clone refreshed at PRELUDE Step 0a):
  Mode 0 — WIN onboarding: workspace=71, GitHub=71. NO WORK.
  Mode 1 — Expansion queue: NO ACTIONABLE EXPANSIONS.
  Mode 1b — Predictions (in our data file): ALL DONE — but poller has flagged PRED-106 INGESTION GAP for ~25 days as the changes_pending_analysis=1 carry-over.
  Mode 2 — Human notes: NO NOTES.
  Mode 2b — Attention inbox (FUSE): NO ATTENTION ITEMS.
  Mode 3 — Defenses: NO PENDING.
  Mode 4 — Globe fingerprint: ALL DONE.
  Assigned issues: NONE.
  External reports: GH issue #1 (Devilwench positive feedback) already processed across ISS-674/698/955/1448; no new external reports.
  → FIRED on Normal mode targeting the PRED-106 ingestion-gap (the changes_pending_analysis=1 carry-over).

PRED-106 INGESTION:

PRED-106 ("16 Hz Aetheric Field Increases Ca²⁺ Signaling >20%") was registered on the dome predictions.html on 2026-04-04 with SHA256 1a364b8325dd409bb5ae806b43b7ab3523a144ed60ff718945988e3c27dc30c1 and a test window of "April 2026" (interpreted as closing 2026-04-30 EOM, ≈1.5 days from this run). The dome label is PENDING — PROSPECTIVE; the entry was never ingested into our data/predictions.json. The poller has carried this as changes_pending_analysis=1 across multiple polls.

KERNEL OF TRUTH: There is a real 40-year published bioelectromagnetics literature on weak-EM ICR effects on cellular calcium signaling (Blackman 1985, Liboff 1985, Blanchard & Blackman 1994, Adey on chick brain Ca²⁺ efflux). 16 Hz is a recognizable frequency in this corpus and corresponds to Ca²⁺ ICR at a magnetic field of ~21 µT (verified arithmetically: 2e × 21e-6 / (2π × 40 amu) ≈ 16 Hz). So the claim sits adjacent to a real research tradition rather than being pure fabrication.

THREE SUBSTANTIVE ISSUES IDENTIFIED PRE-CLOSURE:
  (1) PHANTOM CITATION — PRED-106 cites WIN-071's Ca²⁺ ICR 16 Hz confirmation as the empirical basis. The dome's own wins.html (fetched 2026-04-29 via curl, 951 lines) lists WIN-001 through WIN-069 — WIN-071 does NOT exist in the published registry. Confirmed counts have remained at 69 across all dome surfaces (title, daily_review.json, ai_manifest.json) throughout the 25-day ingestion-gap window.
  (2) MECHANISM HAND-WAVE — the dome replaces the standard B-dependent ICR formula with "ECM aetheric permeability", leaving the resonance condition unfalsifiable. Does not engage with the Kirschvink-style thermal-noise objection (kT >> Lorentz energy at biological temperatures and µT field strengths).
  (3) PROTOCOL GAPS — "Published cell biology literature / replication study" specifies no cell type, no field amplitude, no exposure duration, no Ca²⁺ readout method, no sample size, no randomization, no blinding, no pre-registered statistical test. The <5% falsification threshold is operationally empty without a pre-specified N and α.

VERDICT: pending. Window closes 2026-04-30 EOM. POST-CLOSURE RUBRIC pre-flagged for the decider — (i) silent drop → silent_ignore (mirrors PRED-105 trajectory); (ii) pass-mark via citing pre-existing Liboff/Blackman literature → recycled / standard_physics-with-disputed-mechanism; (iii) genuine pre-registered replication with N, α, blinded counts → assess on merit. Time-sensitive: true.

OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT (per operator user-preference): four counter-attacks anticipated and addressed in anticipated_objections — (1) WIN-071 might be a planned/forthcoming WIN — but it has not appeared across 25 days of the ingestion-gap window and confirmed counts have stayed at 69; (2) Liboff/Blackman literature IS the "confirmation" — that downgrades to recycled, captured in rubric (ii); (3) Kirschvink thermal-noise objection is itself contested — acknowledged (Pilla 2007 alternative coupling theories), but the dome does not specify which alternative mechanism it proposes, so mechanism remains underspecified; (4) why ingest at all if window closes in 1.5 days — to close the 25-day-old changes_pending_analysis carry-over and stake the phantom-WIN-071 finding pre-closure (not as post-hoc rationalization).

NEWSWORTHY: false (phantom WIN-071 is a quiet internal-consistency finding, not a one-off headline; would clear the bar if a pattern of phantom citations across multiple PREDs is later surfaced).

OUTPUTS:
  - monitor/analyst/expansions/PRED-assessment-PRED-106.json (11,637 bytes; suggested_entry + assessment + phantom_citation_flag + 4 anticipated_objections; validated)
  - monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-PRED-106-ingestion.json (2,587 bytes; new_issue type; severity medium; recheck dates 2026-04-30, 2026-05-01, 2026-05-04; validated)
  Synced to FUSE workspace AND analyst clone; workspace-sync universal-pusher will commit on next cycle.

Next-up if no fresh changes lands: idle Mode 4 (globe fingerprint) — currently ALL DONE per tracker. Otherwise wait for new chgs / EXPs / attention items. PRED-105 enters d=11.96+ past close at next poll; consider tacit_withdrawal at d=14 (2026-05-02) if dome remains silent.

Action: (a) create ISS-NNNN tracking issue (next_id 1742+), (b) add suggested_entry from PRED-assessment-PRED-106.json into data/predictions.json's entries[] array (structurally complete; mirrors PRED-105 schema), (c) clear changes_pending_analysis=1 (PRED-106 ingestion was the carry-over), (d) tighten test_window_closes string to '2026-04-30' so Step 10b stops false-positive imminent on EOM. Run tests/run.sh on integration. After integration close the tracking ISS as 'resolved by ingestion'. The phantom WIN-071 finding can either be folded into the entry's notes field (as drafted) or split into a separate ISS for tracking — analyst recommends keeping it in the notes field unless we later find a pattern of phantom citations.

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE — curmudgeon: c1 spot-check on the ingested PRED-106 entry — verify that (i) the suggested_entry was committed verbatim or with annotated decider edits, (ii) the phantom_citation_flag is preserved in the notes field or the linked issue, (iii) the test_window_closes was tightened to 2026-04-30, (iv) no other PRED entries have similarly phantom WIN-NNN citations (worth a sweep if c1 has slack).
